package handler

import (
	"net/http"
	"time"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for column_name_literals_duplicated_outside_repository.
// Rule intent: Repeated column-name strings outside repository or query packages.
// Anti-pattern: Raw column literals like "deleted_at IS NULL" appear in both handler and service layers.

type Article struct {
	ID        uint      `gorm:"primaryKey"`
	Title     string    `gorm:"column:title"`
	Content   string    `gorm:"column:content"`
	Published bool      `gorm:"column:published"`
	DeletedAt time.Time `gorm:"column:deleted_at"`
	CreatedAt time.Time `gorm:"column:created_at"`
}

// Handler layer using raw column literals
func GetActiveArticlesHandler(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	var articles []Article
	db.Where("deleted_at IS NULL").
		Where("published = ?", true).
		Where("created_at > ?", time.Now().AddDate(0, -1, 0)).
		Find(&articles)
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, articles)
}

func SearchArticlesHandler(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	query := c.Query("q")
	var articles []Article
	db.Where("deleted_at IS NULL").
		Where("title LIKE ? OR content LIKE ?", "%"+query+"%", "%"+query+"%").
		Find(&articles)
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, articles)
}

// Service layer also using the same raw column literals
func GetArticleByID(id uint, db *gorm.DB) (*Article, error) {
	var article Article
	err := db.Where("id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL", id).First(&article).Error
	return &article, err
}

func ArchiveArticle(id uint, db *gorm.DB) error {
	return db.Model(&Article{}).
		Where("id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL", id).
		Update("deleted_at", time.Now()).Error
}
